refinement_sections.content: We need to figure out if our new product is selling better than a certain amount we think is good. This means we need to compare the average sales of our product to this benchmark. I guess we have to look at the sales data from the last three months.
We should gather the sales data from our sales team for the last three months. This data will help us know how much of the product was sold during this time.

I think we need to create two statements: one that says the average sales are higher than the benchmark (this is the alternative hypothesis), and one that says the average sales are not higher (this is the null hypothesis). I need to find out what the benchmark number is.
I think we should use a t-test because we are comparing the average of our sales to a single number (the benchmark). But I'm not sure how to do this correctly.
We have to make some assumptions like the sales data is normally distributed. I think we should also check if the data has outliers because they might change the results.

Finally, I need to write a report that explains what we did, what we found, and what we recommend based on the test results. I should also mention any assumptions we made and how they could affect what we conclude.
